classes.name: Bacterial Leaf Blight
classes.description: A serious bacterial disease caused by Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae that causes wilting and yellowing of leaves.
treatment: Use resistant varieties in future planting

classes.name: Brown Spot
classes.description: A fungal disease caused by Bipolaris oryzae that appears as brown spots on leaves and can cause significant yield losses.
treatment: Apply fungicides containing mancozeb or propiconazole

classes.name: Leaf Blast
classes.description: A devastating fungal disease caused by Magnaporthe oryzae that creates diamond-shaped lesions on leaves.
treatment: Ensure proper plant spacing for air circulation

classes.name: Neck Blast
classes.description: A severe form of blast disease affecting the neck (panicle base) of rice plants, leading to white or gray panicles.
treatment: Apply systemic fungicides at booting stage
Use tricyclazole or isoprothiolane as preventive treatment

classes.name: Sheath Blight
classes.description: A soilborne fungal disease caused by Rhizoctonia solani that affects the sheath and leaves of rice plants.

classes.name: Tungro
classes.description: A viral disease transmitted by green leafhoppers that causes stunting and yellow-orange discoloration of leaves.
treatment: Control green leafhopper vector using insecticides

classes.name: Healthy
classes.description: No disease detected. The rice plant appears healthy with normal green coloration and no visible disease symptoms.
treatment: Keep field clean of debris and weeds
